title: Manipulation of magnetic skyrmions by non-uniform electric fields
authors: N. I. Simchuck, I. S. Burmistrov, S. S. Apostoloff
abstract: Magnetic skyrmions are topologically protected spin textures in ferromagnetic materials that hold great promise for both classical information storage and processing, as well as for fault-tolerant quantum computing. Realizing practical skyrmion-based devices demands an energy-efficient and precise method for their flexible manipulation. In this paper, we theoretically propose such a tool, leveraging the magnetoelectric effect induced by a localized electric field generated by one or several charged tips. Combining complementary numerical simulations and analytical approaches, we develop a consistent theory describing the stability and dynamics of Néel-type skyrmions under the influence of the electric field from a charged tip. Specifically, we demonstrate that the electric field can create, drive, and annihilate skyrmions of both chiralities, as well as more complex textures such as skyrmioniums and target skyrmions. We identify several distinct dynamical regimes of skyrmion motion near the tip and map them onto a phase diagram. Finally, we discuss the feasibility of a practical device capable of controlled skyrmion manipulation based on this principle.
summary: This paper proposes using localized electric fields from charged tips to precisely control magnetic skyrmions. The researchers show that this method can create, move, and destroy various spin textures, offering a potential path toward energy-efficient spintronic devices.
main_problem: Developing an energy-efficient and precise method for the flexible manipulation of magnetic skyrmions for information storage and computing.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that localized electric fields from charged tips can create, drive, and annihilate Néel-type skyrmions and complex textures like skyrmioniums.
method: The study combines complementary numerical simulations and analytical approaches to develop a consistent theory of skyrmion stability and dynamics.
model_or_system: The system consists of Néel-type magnetic skyrmions in ferromagnetic materials subjected to a localized electric field induced by one or several charged tips via the magnetoelectric effect.
key_observables: Skyrmion stability, dynamics (motion, creation, annihilation), and the identification of distinct dynamical regimes.
important_parameters: Electric field strength/geometry from the tip, skyrmion chirality, and the phase diagram of motion near the tip.
main_assumptions: The manipulation relies on the presence of the magnetoelectric effect in the ferromagnetic material.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of skyrmion manipulation, proposes a theoretical tool using electric fields, develops a theory using numerical and analytical methods, presents a phase diagram of dynamical regimes, and discusses practical device feasibility.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
